titleStmt.title: SII 16.24: original edition by H. K. Narasimhaswami and G. S. Gai
No. 24. (A.R. No. 526 of 1906.) KADIRI, KADIRI TALUK, ANANTAPUR DISTRICT.

summary.p: On a slab set up below a peepul tree in the court-yard of the Lakshmīnarasiṁhasvāmin temple.
This is dated Śaka 1339, Hēmalaṁba, Phālguṇa śu. 15, Thursday corresponding to 1418 A.D., February 20. The weekday, however, was Sunday.

This damaged inscription seems to record the decision (details lost), of the merchant community in a dispute between the Mālas, Mādigas and the rytus. The king is called Chikkarāya Vīra Kumāra Dēvarāya, (son) of Vīra Harihararāya.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svastiśrī jayābhyudaya śakavarṣaṁbu
lu 1339 Agunēṁṭi hemaḷaṁbi
saṁvatsara phālguṇa śu 15 gu śrīma
drājādhirāja rājaparamēsvara Arirāyavi
bhāḷa paradaḷabhayaṁkkara bhāsegeta
puvarāyaragaṁḍa rājādhirāja
rājaparamēsara śrīvīra hariha
rarāyara cikkarāya śrīvīra ku
māradēvarāya mahārāyalu .
śrīvijayānagara paṭṭaṇaṁbuna
nu prathivirājyaṁ sēyucuṁḍaṁgānu
śrīmadaurēśvaradēvara ( gaurēśvaradē
vara ) divyaśrīpādapadmārādhaku
layina [saṁtativāru mākulōnaI]
na yeṁbhayārudēśāla ḍōluga
la mukhyamayina sama stapekkaṁḍru
nnu śrīkadiri nārasiṁhyadēva
ra mukhamaṁṭapamaṁdu vajrasiṁ
hvāsanārūḍhulai kūrcuṁḍi dha(ṁ)
rmmādhaṁrmmālu vicāriṁcagānu mā
lalunnu mādugulunnu raitulunnu pa
rāmarśiṁci daṁḍaṁveṭṭi viṁnapaṁsē
yagānu viṁnnapaṁ vini sēsina niṁ
rnayamu mālavāriki jellē pannu lēku
ṁḍāsula
kaṁdulamōpu guṟapu kasavu cira
mōpulō marugaḍa
ṭumālu sēsi vūḷigamu mādugulu
I peṁḍlipaṁdiṭi kaṁbhālu nālugu vā
radākānu vāru sēsē vūḷigaṁ mō
vulu yicci mī kulācaraṇaluṁnu
badly damaged
śrīśrīśrī .
Digital edition of SII 16.24 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
